commands.review_summary:
The branch imports the existing OpenAI/Codex runtime-provider resolver into embedded compaction target resolution and adds two regression tests for OpenAI Codex OAuth and non-OpenAI providers.

PR surface: Source +15, Tests +24. Total +39 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main source preserves provider=openai with authProfileId=openai-codex:* in the no-override compaction resolver, and linked 2026.5.22 comments include live logs showing the matching OpenAI API-key failure shape.

target.title: fix(pi-embedded): route openai+codex auth through openai-codex on compaction fallback (#86373)
target.body:
Implements the fix proposed inline in #86373.

## Problem

The normal embedded Pi run path already normalizes `provider=openai` + `authProfileId=openai-codex:*` to the `openai-codex` runtime provider via `resolveSelectedOpenAIPiRuntimeProvider`. But `resolveEmbeddedCompactionTarget` returned the caller provider directly when no `compaction.model` override was configured, leaving the compaction/context-engine fallback in an inconsistent state:

- `provider = openai`
- `authProfileId = openai-codex:…`
- `model = gpt-*`

That state enters the plain OpenAI API-key path and fails with `No API key found for provider openai`, even though the active session is using Codex OAuth. Most visible after a stale Codex app-server thread binding, because the fallback is then used for compaction.

## Fix

Apply the same `resolveSelectedOpenAIPiRuntimeProvider` routing in the compaction target resolver so the main and compaction paths agree on the runtime provider. The override-present branch is unchanged.

Added regression tests:
- `openai + openai-codex:default` resolves to `openai-codex` and preserves the auth profile
- non-openai providers (`anthropic`) are unchanged

Closes #86373
target.author: sanjays2402
